title: BEM-923 worker request
objective:
Create file governance/audit/mailbox/to_curator/BEM923_SYSTEM_PROTOCOL_V3_PROVIDER_MODEL_REQUEST.md with content # BEM-923 SYSTEM PROTOCOL V3 PROVIDER MODEL REQUEST
Status: SENT_TO_CURATOR
Route: External GPT auditor -> Curator -> Worker -> Curator -> external mailbox
Objective: prepare a new full-system protocol version for evolving the system to working state, correcting BEM-921 by adding the provider model of internal contours.
Operator clarification:
- The previous protocol described logical roles but missed the provider layer.
- The system already had a picture of providers and primary/reserve contours.
- Internal contour is not only Analyst/Auditor/Executor; it is logical roles plus provider assignment, failover policy, GitHub Actions/runner activation, exchange files, event log and proof.
Mandatory provider model:
- Primary internal contour uses GPT Analyst, Claude Auditor and Claude Executor.
- Internal contour algorithm remains iterative: Analyst proposal -> Auditor approves/rejects -> Executor runs approved proposal -> Executor returns result -> Auditor approves result or sends revision through Analyst.
- Reserve contour is used when Claude limits or availability block Claude Auditor/Executor.
- Reserve provider for Claude roles is GPT-Codex/Codex route.
- Failover must be recorded in event_log and proof: original provider, fallback provider, reason, trace_id, role, contour_id.
- GitHub Actions and codex-runner are activation/proof mechanisms, not logical roles.
- Exchange files must carry both logical_role and provider_role/provider_id.
Required protocol sections:
1. Full-system goal and current raw state.
2. v1.7 plan/fact/gap plus Claude requirements.
3. Logical architecture: objects, elements, internal contours, workspace, vertical/horizontal links.
4. Provider architecture: provider registry, primary contour, reserve contour, failover conditions.
5. Internal contour algorithm with providers at each step.
6. Required registries: elements, contours, providers, provider policy, failover policy, rules, experience.
7. Prompt architecture: prompts describe role; provider policy says who executes it.
8. Roadmap to working system with acceptance criteria and proof.
9. Operator gates: real secrets/tokens, production webhook, schedule/daemon, issue #31, paid API, LLM runtime, real pilot choices.
10. Claude re-audit criteria.
Worker must verify whether this closes the gap raised by the operator and flag any contradictions.
Expected status: APPROVED_WITH_NOTES or BLOCKED_WITH_REASON.
No issue comments.
